title: Houses for Rent in Cleveland with a Garage
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Cleveland with a Garage
metaDescription: Find houses for rent in Cleveland with a garage. See typical rent ranges, why garages are common on Cleveland lots, and how to book a showing.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent cleveland with garage
answerBox: A detached garage is a common feature on Cleveland's older single-family and duplex lots, typically set off a rear alley. Rent Finder Cleveland manages 90+ rental homes across Greater Cleveland, some with a garage, and every home accepts Section 8 vouchers — book a showing to ask about a specific property's parking.

sections.h2: Can you find a house for rent with a garage in Cleveland?
body: Yes — a detached garage is a common feature of Cleveland's early-to-mid-20th-century housing stock, where standard city lots were laid out with a rear alley and a garage separate from the house rather than an attached one. Garage condition, size (single vs. two-car), and whether it's included in the rental all vary by property, so it's a feature to confirm on any specific listing.

We manage **90+ rental homes** across Greater Cleveland, and a number of them include a garage or dedicated off-street parking. [Book a showing](/p/book-showing) and let us know a garage matters to you, or [start an application](/apply) once you've found the right home.

sections.h2: Why does off-street or garage parking matter in Cleveland?
body: Beyond convenience, covered parking is a practical consideration in a region that sees roughly 60–70 inches of snow a season, with heavier lake-effect totals on the city's higher-elevation East side. A garage means less time scraping ice and shoveling out a car before work, and off-street parking generally is simpler on narrow city streets during winter parking bans.

items: **Detached garage:** Most common configuration on older Cleveland lots, accessed from a rear alley
**Single vs. two-car:** Garage size varies by property and lot
**Driveway-only parking:** Some homes have off-street driveway space without an enclosed garage
**Condition:** Garage age and usability (storage vs. functional parking) should be confirmed per listing

sections.h2: What does rent look like for houses with a garage in Cleveland?
body: Rent for a house with a garage follows the same market factors as any Cleveland rental — bedroom count, condition, and neighborhood — rather than a fixed garage premium. Per **Zumper's July 4, 2026 report**, Cleveland's citywide median asking rent is $1,250/mo, with 2-bedroom homes averaging around $1,100 and 3-bedroom homes around $1,350.

rows: Common, often detached off rear alley
Larger lots more likely to include two-car garage

sections.h2: Where are garage homes typically found in Cleveland?
body: Detached garages are especially common throughout Cleveland's East and Southeast side neighborhoods, where most of Rent Finder Cleveland's managed inventory is located — including **Slavic Village (44105)**, **Collinwood (44110)**, **Glenville (44108)**, **Fairfax and Central (44104)**, **Hough (44103)**, and **Buckeye-Shaker (44120)** — plus a smaller number of West-side homes near **Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway (44102)** and **Old Brooklyn (44109)**. These are largely early-20th-century residential blocks originally platted with alley access for garages, a pattern still visible across most of the city today.

For more on rent and transit by area, see our [Cleveland neighborhoods hub](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/), or explore our full [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) pillar page.

sections.h2: Do garage homes accept Section 8 vouchers?
body: Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages — with or without a garage — **accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready**. Voucher holders should know that Ohio has no statewide source-of-income protection law and the City of Cleveland does not require landlords to accept vouchers; only a handful of suburbs (Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale) have local ordinances protecting voucher holders. Learn more on the [CMHA Housing Choice Voucher page](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) or our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/).

sections.h2: How to find a house with a garage
body: Because a garage is a per-property feature rather than a citywide guarantee, the fastest way to find one is to tell us directly what you're looking for. [Book a showing](/p/book-showing) and mention off-street parking or a garage is a priority, and we'll point you toward current listings that match.

faq.q: Are there houses for rent in Cleveland with a garage?
faq.a: Yes, a detached garage is a common feature on Cleveland's older single-family and duplex lots, typically accessed from a rear alley. Garage size and condition vary by property, so it's best to ask about a specific listing. Book a showing and mention a garage is a priority.

faq.q: Why do Cleveland rental houses often have detached garages instead of attached ones?
faq.a: Most of Cleveland's residential blocks were platted in the early-to-mid-20th century with rear alleys, so garages were built detached and accessed from the alley rather than attached to the house. This layout is still common across most of the city's older neighborhoods today.

faq.q: Do garage homes cost more to rent in Cleveland?
faq.a: Not necessarily. Rent is driven mainly by bedroom count, condition, and neighborhood rather than the garage itself. A detached garage is common enough on standard Cleveland lots that it doesn't typically carry a separate price premium on its own.

faq.q: Which Cleveland neighborhoods have more houses with garages?
faq.a: Detached garages are common throughout Cleveland's East and Southeast side neighborhoods — including Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, Fairfax/Central, Hough, and Buckeye-Shaker — where standard single-family lots with alley-accessed garages are the norm, as well as a smaller number of West-side homes.

faq.q: Do garage houses accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Yes. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, regardless of parking type. Voucher holders should confirm current CMHA rules and know that source-of-income protections only apply in a few Cuyahoga County suburbs, not the City of Cleveland itself.
